name: Pozos Artesianos, monte de Carbonero y arte rupestre de Domingo García — circular desde Navas de Oro
region_intro: Mountain biking around Navas De Oro offers diverse terrain through extensive pine forests and the Eresma River valley. The region is characterized by its significant woodlands, primarily maritime and Scots pine, providing numerous shaded trails. Riders can explore varied landscapes, including riverbanks, agricultural fields, and areas featuring several natural lagoons. The topography includes gentle elevation changes suitable for a range of mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Navas De Oro The most popular mountain bike trail is Pozos Artesianos, monte de Carbonero y arte rupestre de Domingo García — circular desde Navas de Oro, a 25.8 miles (41.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 32 minutes to complete. This route explores the local pine forests and historical elements. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Coca Castle – Mudéjar Tower of San Nicolás loop from Coca, a moderate 31.1 miles (50.1 km) path. This trail offers views of historical landmarks and varied natural scenery. Local mountain bikers also love the Nava de la Asunción – Puente de Constanzana loop from Nava de la Asunción, a 43.9 miles (70.6 km) trail leading through the Eresma River valley, often completed in about 6 hours 2 minutes. Mountain biking around Navas De Oro is defined by extensive pine forests, the Eresma River valley, and natural lagoons. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Navas De Oro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Navas De Oro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Navas de Oro?
A: There are over 25 mountain bike trails around Navas de Oro listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find options ranging from easy to difficult, primarily traversing the extensive pine forests and the Eresma River valley.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Navas de Oro?
A: The terrain around Navas de Oro is diverse, characterized by extensive pine forests, particularly maritime and Scots pine, which provide many shaded trails. You'll also encounter routes along the Eresma River valley, agricultural fields, and areas featuring natural lagoons. The topography generally includes gentle elevation changes, making it suitable for a range of mountain biking experiences.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Navas de Oro?
A: Yes, Navas de Oro offers a few easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. These routes typically feature less challenging terrain and elevation, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty of the pine forests and river landscapes at a comfortable pace.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Navas de Oro?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes in Navas de Oro are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084778596/pozos-artesianos-monte-de-carbonero-y-arte-rupestre-de-domingo-garcia-circular-desde-navas-de-oro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozos Artesianos, monte de Carbonero y arte rupestre de Domingo García — circular desde Navas de Oro&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 41.6 km loop that explores local pine forests and historical elements.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while mountain biking in Navas de Oro?
A: While mountain biking in Navas de Oro, you can explore several natural and historical features. The region is known for its extensive pine forests and the picturesque Eresma River valley. You might also encounter natural lagoons like Laguna de la Vega and Laguna de la Magdalena, which are important stops for migratory birds. For historical sights, consider routes that pass by the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813983" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coca Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3711912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mudéjar Tower of San Nicolás&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass by historical sites?
A: Absolutely. Several routes incorporate historical elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coca Castle – Mudéjar Tower of San Nicolás loop from Coca&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 50.1 km trail that offers views of significant historical landmarks. Another route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084778596/pozos-artesianos-monte-de-carbonero-y-arte-rupestre-de-domingo-garcia-circular-desde-navas-de-oro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozos Artesianos, monte de Carbonero y arte rupestre de Domingo García — circular desde Navas de Oro&lt;/a&gt;, also explores local historical elements within the pine forests.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Navas de Oro?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Navas de Oro is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is milder and more pleasant. The extensive pine forests offer good shade during warmer months, but summer temperatures can be high. Winter can be suitable, but always check local weather conditions, as some trails might be affected by rain or colder temperatures.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nava de la Asunción – Puente de Constanzana loop from Nava de la Asunción&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 70.6 km trail leading through the Eresma River valley, typically taking over 6 hours to complete. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31480846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carbonero el Mayor loop from Nava de la Asunción&lt;/a&gt;, which is 84.8 km long.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Navas de Oro?
A: The mountain bike trails in Navas de Oro are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Riders often praise the quiet woodlands, the varied natural scenery, and the well-maintained paths that allow for an immersive experience in nature.

Q: Are there any facilities or rest areas along the mountain bike routes?
A: While specific facilities vary by route, some areas offer amenities. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3531321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miguel Ibáñez Rest Area and Fountain&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight that could be incorporated into a ride, providing a spot to rest and refill water. Many routes also pass through or near small towns where you can find services.

Q: Can I access mountain bike trails in Navas de Oro using public transport?
A: While Navas de Oro itself is a municipality, accessing specific trailheads directly by public transport might require some planning. It's advisable to check local bus schedules to nearby towns like Coca or Nava de la Asunción, from which many routes originate or pass through. However, having your own transport often provides more flexibility for reaching starting points.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails in Navas de Oro suitable for families?
A: Yes, Navas de Oro offers trails suitable for families, especially the easier routes that traverse the flat sections of the pine forests or along the Eresma River. These trails provide a safe and enjoyable environment for families to cycle together, allowing them to experience the natural beauty without excessive difficulty.
tips: From this point we have a beautiful view of the Constanzana Bridge that allows us to cross the Eresma river. A good place to rest and recover energy and why not? take a nice picture.
Next to the hermitage of San Isidro we come across the outdoor rock art station of Domingo García. It is a set of engravings that range from the Upper Paleolithic to the Contemporary Age.

waypoints.name: Domingo García Outdoor Rock Art Site
